target.name: Kairos Power
target.founder: Mike Laufer, Edward Blandford, Per Peterson

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $1.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Nuclear Energy. Range: $1.0B - $4.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: TerraPower
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Late Stage), post-money $4B+

data.narrative:
Kairos Power is a late-stage nuclear energy company with $500M+ raised and a current post-money valuation of $1.6B. The peer universe comprises 10 comparables across public and private markets: 3 publicly traded firms (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 7 private peers including TerraPower, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, and Zap Energy, all operating in advanced nuclear or related frontier energy segments.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applied a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of late-stage advancement, yielding $1.9B. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector comparables at similar valuation tiers—TerraPower ($4.0B), X-Energy ($2.0B), Nano Nuclear Energy ($1.5B), and Zap Energy ($1.0B)—produced a median of $1.8B, with a range of $1.0B to $4.0B. Both methods converged within a narrow band, supporting reliability of the estimate.

Triangulation across the two methods yields a valuation range of $1.5B (low) to $2.2B (high), with a median of $1.8B. This range sits modestly above the current $1.6B mark, consistent with typical late-stage momentum. Primary uncertainty stems from the wide dispersion within the peer set ($1.0B–$4.0B), reflecting nascent sector maturity and variable stage distribution among comparables. Commercialization risk and regulatory pathway clarity remain key value drivers not captured in relative multiples.
